title: Skopeo picks wrong credentials from .docker/config.json on copy
body:
I am trying to copy an image from one private registry to another one with 
```
skopeo copy docker://some-docker-registry.com/source-registry/my-image:1.0.0 docker://src-docker-registry.com/dest-registry/my-image:1.0.0
```
This fails because, skopeo tries to authenticate to `source-registry` with the credentials from `dest-registry`. 

The debug log shows
```
DEBU[0000] GET https://some-docker-registry.com/.../token?account=some-other-name&scope=repository%3Adest-registry%2Fimage%3Apull 
```

I think it might be important here that the registry urls differ only in the last path segment.

The `.docker/config.json` contains two entries
```json
{
  "auths": {
    "some-docker-registry.com/source-registry": {
      "username": "some-name",
      "password": "***",
      "email": "some-name@example.com",
      "auth": "***"
    },
    "some-docker-registry.com/dest-registry": {
      "username": "some-other-name",
      "password": "***",
      "email": "some-other-name@example.com",
      "auth": "***"
    }
  }
}
```
I can successfully inspect both registries individually, when heaving separate config.json files for each registry. 

I think this should either be fixed or one should be able to pass separate config files for source and destination like one can do with the cert paths. This would be something like 
```
skopeo copy --src-authfile some-config.json --dest-authfile some-other-config.json  ...
```

comments.body: @dastrobu thanks for sending this along and I like the thought of the `--src/dest-authfile` options.  I don't know if it's palatable to you or not, but say your authfile had the creds for the source directory.  You could use the `--dest-creds` option in the `skopeo copy` command line and get it to work that way for now at least.  Or use the `--src-creds` option if you'd the destination creds in your authfile.

My first instinct is to model the actual situation accurately in the account / permission system: i.e. give the principal (personal account, service account) a _single_ account that is simultaneously assigned the puller role for one repository and the pusher role for the other one. That would transparently and accurately record the possible accesses, and allow for auditing of the granted privileges. Having two separate accounts available to the same principal without any trace in the system is less transparent. OTOH I know very little about OpenShift, it may not be possible to set the system up that way.

@mtrmac using the same user (service account) for the pulling and pushing can be technically an option. However, there are good reasons to keep these separate and discussing the organizational pros and cons of using the same or different credentials for pulling and pushing might be beyond the scope of this issue. 
There are some workarounds (use different hosts as pointed out above) or use the same credentials, as you pointed out. I think it was definitely good to have this discussion, in case someone rans into the same issue. 

Regarding a "fix" I see three options: 
 1. do nothing and let people use the workarounds as pointed out.
 2. add support for the `--src-authfile` and `--dest-authfile` flags.
 3. change the way a docker config is interpreted, by first reading the host only (current state) and in case there are several credentials for the same host, look at the next path segment after the host to resolve any ambiguity.

I can live with all options. The second option would be very transparent but might be a bit confusing  if there are so many options to pass credentials (`--authfile`, `--src-authfile`, `--dest-authfile`, `--src-creds`, `--des-creds`). The last option would be most convenient from a user perspective but since it cannot be fixed inside skopeo would be out of scope for this issue.

FWIW, I’d prefer option 2: the single `--authfile` option is actually a unique corner case in Skopeo’s option processing, so using the prefixed variant like other `imageOptions` would be consistent with the rest of the code (OTOH, sure, the unique corner case would remain).
@TomSweeneyRedHat and @mtrmac I create a [pr](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/798) implementation the additional flags (option 2), let me know what you think.
